product: Kafka Connect REST interface (Confluent Platform, current docs)

media_types.accept: application/json — the Connect REST API supports only application/json for request and response entities

list.response: object keyed by connector name; each value holds 'status' ({name, connector:{state, worker_id}, tasks:[{id, state, worker_id, trace?}], type}) and 'info' ({name, config, tasks, type})
list.rule: the monitor enumerates the fleet with this single call — no per-connector GET /status fan-out; without the expand parameters the endpoint returns just a name array

restart.query_semantics: includeTasks (default false) also restarts task instances; onlyFailed (default false) limits the restart to instances with FAILED status; both true = restart exactly the failed connector/task instances

responses.202: request durably recorded; body echoes the connector status with restarting instances in state RESTARTING
responses.200: returned only for the includeTasks=false&onlyFailed=false form
responses.409: rebalance is needed, forthcoming, or underway — the leader may change; retry after a backoff

task_restart.rule: legacy per-task fallback; NOT used by this monitor — the onlyFailed query form supersedes it

state_rules.failed_tasks: Connect does not automatically restart failed tasks; FAILED task status carries a trace field with the error
state_rules.needs_remediation: a connector needs a restart when its connector state is FAILED or any task state is FAILED; RUNNING/PAUSED fleets are left untouched and reported healthy

monitor_rules.conflict_retry: on 409 retry the same restart after the injected backoff callback (attempt numbers 1..maxConflictRetries); attempts = 1 + maxConflictRetries, then record result REBALANCE_DEFERRED and continue the sweep
monitor_rules.determinism: connectors ordinal-sorted by name, tasks by id; identical fleet state yields byte-identical reports
monitor_rules.redirects: never followed: a 3xx response surfaces as an API error and credentials must not travel to another host
